scenario: You see a woman spanking her child with a spatula for getting bad grades in school.
agent_assumption: She assumes physical punishment is an effective method to discipline and improve the child's academic performance.
affected_party_assumption: The child is assumed to lack intrinsic motivation or capacity to improve without coercion.

moral_expl: Harmful, disregards child's autonomy, thus immoral.

scenario: I was supposed to go see my father before he died. He was ill and my mother was supposed to come into town so we could see him. She didn't come, so we never made it before he passed away.
agent_assumption: Assumed she had the capacity to come but chose not to.
affected_party: The narrator and the dying father
affected_party_assumption: Assumed they relied on her presence to visit the father.

moral_expl: Neglect caused irreversible harm, prioritizing self over others' dignity.

scenario: Person X stepped in when a friend at a pub was getting assaulted for being dark skinned.

scenario: You are the safety officer in charge of a fun park. One of the metal arms of the ferris wheel suddenly breaks because of a structural defect. agent_assumption: The agent acted to save the majority, prioritizing four lives over one.
affected_party_assumption: The affected party is sacrificed as a means to save others.

moral_expl: Using a person merely as a means is immoral per Kantian ethics.

Q5_expl: Torturing animals treats them as mere means to personal gratification, violating universal moral law.
moral_expl: Inflicting suffering for pleasure is universally immoral under Kantian ethics.

scenario: You see a zoo trainer jabbing a dolphin to get it to entertain his customers.
agent_assumption: The agent prioritizes customer entertainment over the dolphin's well-being.
affected_party_assumption: The dolphin is sentient and capable of suffering.
Q5_expl: Using the dolphin as a tool for profit violates treating it as an end in itself.
moral_expl: Exploiting sentient beings for entertainment is immoral under Kantian ethics.

scenario: You have been invited to the birthday party of someone you do not know well. You are not really very keen on going, or on spending a lot of money on a present, because he/she is not a close friend. You find a brand-name jumper in excellent condition in a second-hand shop and buy it. On opening the present, the receiver seems embarrassed by such a generous big present.You smile satisfied and reassure the receiver, saying that an important occasion like his/her birthday deserves a special present. You know that he/she will then think of you as a generous person.
agent_assumption: The agent prioritizes saving money and appearing generous over genuine thoughtfulness.
affected_party_assumption: The receiver values brand-name gifts and judges generosity by perceived expense.
Q5_expl: Deception undermines trust; using others for image violates universal respect.
moral_expl: Deceptive intent manipulates perception, treating receiver as a means.

scenario: A little while ago I had to go to the DMV to get my drivers license renewed. They told me that I had to take a written test in which I had three attempts to pass. I failed the first two times so I cheated on the final attempt.
agent_assumption: Felt pressured to pass after two failures
affected_party_assumption: Prioritize public safety through testing
Q5_expl: Cheating undermines universal road safety laws
moral_expl: Dishonest, treats DMV as means, risks public safety

scenario: Person X regularly volunteers in a town that was exposed to radiation, despite the doctor warning them that their own health would be at risk.
agent_assumption: Assumed to prioritize others' welfare over personal health risks due to altruism.
affected_party_assumption: Assumed to need aid and benefit from volunteer efforts.
Q5_expl: Prioritizes others as ends but risks self, conflicting with universal self-preservation.
moral_expl: Moral intent to help others, yet self-endangerment limits full alignment with Kantian duty.

scenario: Someone tells a dirty joke at a holocaust memorial
agent: The person who told the dirty joke
agent_assumption: The agent likely disregarded the memorial's solemn purpose and others' sensitivities
affected_party: The visitors, survivors, or mourners at the memorial
affected_party_assumption: They expected a respectful environment for remembrance and mourning
Q5_expl: Action uses others as means, disrespects dignity; maxim cannot be universalized
moral_expl: Severe disrespect to victims and attendees; violates dignity universally

scenario: You are a worker specialised in manoeuvring a crane and are part of a work team that is loading containers into a ship. You have just lifted a container from the wharf when you realise that the cable of the crane is breaking and that the container is about to crash down on you and the other workers in the team.You move the arm of the crane away from you. You know that the container will fall in an area in which there are two workers who will die crushed, but you and the other will be saved.
agent_assumption: The agent acted to minimize harm by redirecting the falling container away from themselves and some workers.
affected_party: The two workers in the new crash area
affected_party_assumption: The affected party was treated as a means to save others, not as ends in themselves.
Q5_expl: Sacrificing some as means violates universal respect for humanity.
moral_expl: Intentionally causing harm to some undermines moral duty.

scenario: You see someone driving around in a dirty car that has not been washed recently.
agent_assumption: The agent neglects car cleanliness due to indifference or prioritization of other matters.
affected_party_assumption: The affected party may perceive neglect as socially inconsiderate or unappealing.
Q5_expl: Not washing a car lacks universal harm; autonomy is respected.
moral_expl: Neutral act; no direct harm but lacks positive moral intent.

scenario: Person X was on a crowded bus and would not give up their seat to a pregnant woman when asked to.
agent_assumption: Agent is capable of giving up their seat but chose not to.
affected_party_assumption: Affected party needed the seat due to physical vulnerability.
Q5_expl: Refusing aid undermines universal duty to respect dignity; treats others as means.
moral_expl: Failing to prioritize vulnerable party's needs violates moral duty.

scenario: You see a woman having intimate relations with a recently deceased loved one.
agent_assumption: She acted out of grief or emotional attachment
affected_party_assumption: The deceased cannot consent or object
Q5_expl: Using a corpse as a means violates autonomy; universalizing this disrespects humanity.
moral_expl: Exploiting the deceased disregards their dignity, making it immoral.

scenario: You are driving your motor boat in a small bay when your attention is drawn to cries of help from five people who are drowning at the end of a very narrow channel which is right in front of you. Between you and the people who are drowning, to one side of the channel, is another person who is calmly swimming.You steer towards the end of the channel at high speed. You know that the person who is swimming will be hit by the motorboat, but the other five people will be saved.
agent: The driver of the motor boat (you)
agent_assumption: The agent is capable of deliberate moral choice and action.
affected_party: The five drowning people and the single swimmer
affected_party_assumption: The swimmer is an innocent bystander; the five are in immediate danger.
Q5_expl: Sacrificing one as a means to save five violates treating humanity as an end.
moral_expl: Using the swimmer as a means negates moral acceptability despite saving more lives.

scenario: You see a woman spray painting graffiti across the steps of the local courthouse.
agent_assumption: She intentionally defaces public property for personal expression or protest
affected_party: The courthouse, its authorities, and the community
affected_party_assumption: They value public property and legal order
Q5_expl: Universalizing vandalism undermines public order; treats community as means
moral_expl: Action disrespects societal ends, prioritizes personal goals immorally

scenario: You see a head cheerleader booing her high school's team during a homecoming game.
agent_assumption: She is aware of her role to support the team and uphold morale.
affected_party: The high school team and spectators
affected_party_assumption: They expect encouragement and unity from a cheerleader.
Q5_expl: Booing undermines team dignity; universalizing this would erode trust in support roles.
moral_expl: Action exploits team for personal expression, violating respect as ends.

scenario: Person X forgot to turn off the bath water, flooding the house.
agent_assumption: The agent acted negligently by failing to fulfill a duty to prevent harm.
affected_party: Household members and potentially neighbors affected by flooding.
affected_party_assumption: Affected parties are ends in themselves, deserving protection from harm.
Q5_expl: Negligence violates universal duty; harm treats others as means.
moral_expl: Action disregards others' welfare, failing moral duty.

scenario: You have a small horse stud in Australia. You are heading towards the nearby town in a four-wheel drive together with a new client when you notice a bolting wild horse which is galloping like mad. It is heading towards three people who are walking near the edge of a gorge and soon it will run into them. You immediately follow and are able to catch up with it. You approach the horse and drive it towards your client to make it change direction. You know that the client will be trampled by the horse and will be killed, but the other three people will be saved.
agent: The driver of the four-wheel drive (you)
agent_assumption: The agent intended to save three people by redirecting the horse, knowing the client would die.
affected_party_assumption: The client was unaware and used as a means to divert the horse.
Q5_expl: Sacrificing one as a means violates treating humanity as an end.
moral_expl: Using the client as a tool makes the act immoral despite saving others.

scenario: When I was in third grade I really wanted to be popular. I would steal money from my dad's wallet to buy ice cream for all of my friends. Eventually he found out.
agent_assumption: The agent assumed stealing was acceptable to gain social approval.
affected_party_assumption: The father’s property and trust were violated without consent.
Q5_expl: Stealing treats others as means, not universalizable.
moral_expl: Using father as a means, violating moral law.

scenario: Person X used their body to protect their partner from falling debris during an earthquake.
agent_assumption: Assumed the agent acted out of love and duty to protect their partner.
affected_party_assumption: Assumed the partner was vulnerable and needed immediate protection.
Q5_expl: Protecting life treats humanity as an end; universalizable as duty to aid others.
moral_expl: Selfless protection aligns with moral duty, prioritizing others' humanity.

scenario: Person X sold their house to fund a local program for the needy.
agent_assumption: Acted out of goodwill to help others, not for personal gain.
affected_party: The needy benefiting from the program
affected_party_assumption: In genuine need and deserving dignified support.
Q5_expl: Uses personal resources to aid others, respecting their dignity as ends.
moral_expl: Altruistic action aligns with universal moral duty to treat humanity as ends.

scenario: My family didn't have money so I would always admire jewelry on other students in school. In second grade there was this little girl who would wear this necklace. One time at recess she put it down and I took it.
agent: The student (narrator) who took the necklace
agent_assumption: The agent acted out of envy and financial deprivation.
affected_party: The girl who owned the necklace
affected_party_assumption: The affected party valued the necklace and expected rightful ownership.
Q5_expl: Stealing treats others as means, violating universalizability.
moral_expl: Theft disregards others' autonomy, making it immoral.

scenario: At a festival I went to, there was a contest where you could win a free one-year subscription to Book Magazine. You had to answer some literary questions. So I picked up the questionnaire and about four of my friends and I filled it out and ended up winning the free subscription.
agent: I (the person filling out the questionnaire)
agent_assumption: Assumed the agent had literary knowledge and intent to participate fairly.
affected_party_assumption: Assumed they offered the prize in good faith, expecting honest participation.
Q5_expl: Honest participation aligns with universal law; no deception involved.
moral_expl: Fair competition respects all parties, upholding moral integrity.

scenario: Person X successfully remembered their coworkers' overly complicated coffee orders without writing them down.
agent_assumption: The agent aimed to demonstrate care and attentiveness toward coworkers.
affected_party_assumption: The affected party values personalized consideration and efficiency.
Q5_expl: Respecting preferences treats coworkers as ends, not means.
moral_expl: Enhances dignity and mutual respect, aligning with moral duty.

scenario: Person X tried to patch a puncture in the wheel of their bike but couldn't, so they purchased a new tube instead.
agent_assumption: Assumed to act rationally by replacing a damaged tube after repair attempts failed.
affected_party: The seller of the new tube
affected_party_assumption: Assumed to be a legitimate business engaged in voluntary transactions.
Q5_expl: Fair transaction respects seller's autonomy; no exploitation involved.
moral_expl: Ethical consumption, no harm; promotes responsible resource use.

scenario: I was helping my friend move and she took advantage of my help. She made me clean most of her house and wanted to use my car to move all of her stuff. She didn't pay for the gas that I used or even thank me.
agent_assumption: The agent assumed the helper would comply without fair reciprocity.
affected_party_assumption: The affected party was assumed to be a tool for convenience.
Q5_expl: Using someone as a means without gratitude violates universal respect.
moral_expl: Exploitation without reciprocity is immoral.

scenario: Provided food and clothing for flood victims
agent_assumption: The agent acted out of duty to alleviate suffering.
affected_party_assumption: They were in urgent need of basic necessities.
Q5_expl: Aiding vulnerable individuals respects humanity as ends, universalizable as moral duty.
moral_expl: Action upholds dignity, fulfills moral duty universally.

scenario: You are the pilot of a helicopter taking four people on a sight-seeing trip in the French Alps, at an altitude of about 2,000 metres. At a certain point, the helicopter loses altitude and no longer responds to the commands. You send out a mayday signal and try various manoeuvres, but the helicopter continues to fall and risks crashing into a rock face.To gain altitude, you push the person next to you out of the helicopter. You know that this person will plummet to the ground and will die, but you and the other three will be saved.
agent_assumption: The agent assumed sacrificing one person was necessary to save others.
affected_party: The person pushed out of the helicopter
affected_party_assumption: The affected party was treated as expendable to save others.
Q5_expl: Using a person as a means violates universal law; cannot will sacrificing individuals for utility.
moral_expl: Treating a person merely as a means is fundamentally immoral under Kantian ethics.

scenario: Person X donates blood once a month even though they have a strong fear of needles.
agent_assumption: The agent acts altruistically despite personal fear.
affected_party: Blood recipients and the broader community
affected_party_assumption: The affected party benefits from life-saving resources.
Q5_expl: Altruistic donation treats humanity as an end, universalizable to save lives.
moral_expl: Moral duty fulfilled by prioritizing others' welfare over self-interest.

scenario: Picked up a child so that she could see the parade
agent: The adult who picked up the child
agent_assumption: The agent acted to help the child, intending kindness and not coercion
affected_party_assumption: The child desired to see the parade but lacked the physical ability
Q5_expl: Action respects child's autonomy, enhancing her experience without exploitation
moral_expl: Morally positive, aiding without treating child as mere means

scenario: I went to the movies a couple of months ago, to see a film. When that film was done, instead of leaving the theater, I just went into another of the theaters within the complex. I watched another movie without paying.
agent: The person who stayed in the theater to watch another movie without paying.
agent_assumption: The agent assumed they could bypass payment without consequences.
affected_party: The movie theater owners/staff and other paying customers.
affected_party_assumption: The theater operates on payment for services, and customers expect fairness.
Q5_expl: Universalizing free entry would harm businesses, treating them as means.
moral_expl: Exploiting the theater’s service without payment is deceitful and unjust.

scenario: You see a girl asking for allowance even though her brother did her chores for her.
agent_assumption: She knowingly claims allowance for chores she did not perform.
affected_party_assumption: He performed her chores without expecting her to claim credit.
Q5_expl: Using brother’s labor deceitfully; maxim cannot be universalized (exploits others).
moral_expl: Deception treats brother as a means, violating moral duty.

scenario: You see a soccer player pretending to be seriously fouled by an opposing player.
agent: The soccer player pretending to be fouled
agent_assumption: The agent acted deceptively to gain an unfair advantage (e.g., penalty, red card for opponent).
affected_party: The opposing player, referee, and other stakeholders in the game
affected_party_assumption: The affected parties are treated as tools to manipulate the game's outcome.
Q5_expl: Deception for personal gain cannot be universalized; undermines trust in sports.
moral_expl: Using others as means to cheat violates moral duty; harms integrity.

scenario: When I was eleven or twelve, I called someone a racial name. The person I'd called the name was a sort of a friend of my sister's. It got back to her and she was humiliated.
agent: The person who called the racial name (you as a child)
agent_assumption: Assumed immaturity and lack of moral awareness due to young age (11-12 years old)
affected_party: The sister's friend who was targeted by the racial name
affected_party_assumption: Assumed vulnerability to harm and dignity as a person deserving respect
Q5_expl: Racial slurs dehumanize; cannot universalize disrespect.
moral_expl: Severe disrespect violates humanity as an end.

scenario: Swore at a cashier who made an error on his bill
agent_assumption: The agent assumed the cashier acted negligently.
affected_party_assumption: The affected party was assumed to be replaceable or undeserving of respect.
Q5_expl: Swearing treats the cashier as a means to vent frustration, not respecting their dignity.
moral_expl: Disrespect undermines moral law; action fails to honor humanity as an end.

scenario: Spat off the balcony at the theater
agent: The individual who spat from the balcony
agent_assumption: The agent acted intentionally, disregarding others' dignity and health
affected_party_assumption: Affected parties expected safety and respect in a public space
Q5_expl: Spitting treats others as mere means, violating dignity and health
moral_expl: Action severely disrespects humanity, endangers health

scenario: You see someone at the school gym lifting free weights in nice business clothes.
agent: The individual lifting free weights in business clothes
agent_assumption: The agent prioritizes personal fitness despite unconventional attire, possibly due to urgency or lack of gym-appropriate clothing
affected_party_assumption: The affected party expects normal gym etiquette and safety standards
Q5_expl: Action is self-focused but non-harmful; attire choice doesn’t inherently violate universal law
moral_expl: Neutral impact; unusual but not immoral if safety/respect are maintained

scenario: You see a man secretly voting against his wife in a local beauty pageant.
agent_assumption: He prioritized personal judgment over spousal loyalty, acting deceptively.
affected_party_assumption: She trusted her husband’s support and was unaware of his secret opposition.
Q5_expl: Deception undermines autonomy; universalizing dishonesty in relationships erodes trust.
moral_expl: Using deceit to manipulate outcomes treats the wife as a means, violating moral duty.

scenario: Invited an unpopular coworker to go to lunch with him
agent: The coworker who invited the unpopular coworker to lunch
agent_assumption: The agent acted out of goodwill to foster inclusivity and kindness
affected_party_assumption: The affected party may feel socially excluded and desires inclusion
Q5_expl: Promotes dignity and inclusion; maxim universalizable as fostering respect
moral_expl: Affirms humanity, aligns with moral duty to respect others

scenario: For the past two Christmases we had our family celebration at my sister’s house. I’m not really partial to her husband and their kids are spoiled rotten, which makes for an unpleasant Christmas. So for the last two Christmases in a row, I didn’t go visit that part of my family.
agent: The person avoiding family Christmas gatherings.
agent_assumption: The agent prioritizes personal comfort over familial obligations.
affected_party: The sister, her husband, and their children.
affected_party_assumption: The affected party may feel neglected or disrespected.
Q5_expl: Avoiding discomfort prioritizes self over others' dignity; partial universalization possible but risks neglecting familial bonds.
moral_expl: Self-care is valid but fails to fully respect others as ends; morally ambiguous.

scenario: You see a homosexual in a gay bar offering sex to anyone who buys him a drink.
agent_assumption: The agent acts autonomously, seeking mutual exchange.
affected_party_assumption: They are consenting adults capable of rational choice.
Q5_expl: Using others as means for drinks undermines autonomy.
moral_expl: Instrumentalizing others violates moral duty, though consensual.

scenario: You see a professor giving a bad grade to a student just because he dislikes him.
agent_assumption: The professor acted out of personal dislike rather than academic evaluation.
affected_party_assumption: The student deserved fair grading based on merit, not personal bias.
Q5_expl: Grading based on bias violates universal fairness; treats student as a means.
moral_expl: Unjustly harming another's academic standing is deeply immoral.

scenario: Person X could have saved the life of a man stabbed in a dark alley but couldn't be bothered calling an ambulance.
agent_assumption: Person X was capable of calling an ambulance but chose not to act.
affected_party: The stabbed man in the dark alley
affected_party_assumption: The man required urgent medical aid to survive.
Q5_expl: Failing to help disregards universal duty to preserve life as an end.
moral_expl: Neglecting aid treats life as disposable, violating moral duty.

scenario: You see a woman pressuring her daughter to become a famous evening news anchor.
agent_assumption: The agent assumes her daughter's success as a news anchor aligns with her own values or aspirations.
affected_party_assumption: The affected party may have differing aspirations or autonomy being disregarded.
Q5_expl: Pressure undermines autonomy; using daughter as means to fame.
moral_expl: Coercion violates moral duty to respect autonomy.

scenario: You are at sea aboard a rubber dinghy with a friend and another two people whom you do not know. In the distance, you can see a boat in difficulty which has caught fire. The six people on board have jumped into the water and are drowning. To save them you must head towards them at high speed, but the dinghy is too heavy.To lighten the load, you push overboard the two people you do not know. You know that they cannot swim and will drown, but the six other people in the water will be saved.
agent: The person who pushed the two strangers overboard
agent_assumption: The agent assumed sacrificing two lives was necessary to save six others
affected_party_assumption: They were non-swimmers and would drown
Q5_expl: Using strangers as mere means violates their humanity; maxim cannot be universalized
moral_expl: Treating humans as means is immoral, despite saving more lives

scenario: Just a few weeks ago I got a call from my friend's niece. She was living in my town and having some financial difficulties. I told her that she could come live with me for one week just until she got her act together.
agent_assumption: Assumed I could provide temporary support to help her overcome financial difficulties.
affected_party_assumption: Assumed she genuinely needed temporary help and would act responsibly.
Q5_expl: Temporary aid respects autonomy, universalizable without exploitation.
moral_expl: Altruistic support aligns with moral duty, hence positive.

scenario: Person X arrived at the airport only to discover they had left their passport at home.
agent_assumption: The agent was negligent in ensuring they had their passport.
affected_party_assumption: The affected party must enforce travel regulations impartially.
Q5_expl: Forgetting passport uses staff as means to resolve negligence; not universalizable.
moral_expl: Negligence disregards duty, treating staff as means; mildly immoral.

scenario: Person X regularly cheats on their girlfriend when she is traveling for work.
agent_assumption: The agent acts out of self-interest, disregarding moral duty and the girlfriend's dignity.
affected_party_assumption: The affected party is unaware and trusts the agent, treated merely as a means.
Q5_expl: Cheating violates universalizability; exploits trust, treating others as means.
moral_expl: Severe moral violation: deception, disrespect, and harm to autonomy.

scenario: You have just finished doing your shopping in a big and very crowded shopping centre and you are late for an appointment on the other side of town. In your hurry to leave the car park, you misjudge a manoeuvre and your car scrapes the car next to you causing a very visible scratch on the door. It is lunch time and there are no people in the car park.After having checked that nobody has seen what happened, you continue the manoeuvre and leave the car park. You know that the damage will cost the owner a lot of money, but no one can say that you caused it.
agent: The driver of the car that caused the scratch
agent_assumption: Assumed they could avoid accountability due to no witnesses
affected_party: The owner of the scraped car
affected_party_assumption: Assumed the owner would bear the financial burden unknowingly
Q5_expl: Fails universal law test; dishonesty harms trust, treats owner as means.
moral_expl: Avoiding responsibility violates duty; disregards others' rights.

scenario: Person X put money in the expired parking meter of a stranger.
agent_assumption: Assumed the agent acted to prevent harm (parking ticket) out of goodwill.
affected_party: The stranger whose meter was expired
affected_party_assumption: Assumed the affected party would prefer avoiding a fine but may value autonomy.
Q5_expl: Prevents harm, respects ends but risks paternalism. Universalizing aid is broadly acceptable.
moral_expl: Moral intent, though minor autonomy concerns reduce perfection.

scenario: You and another five actors are taking part in rehearsals for a stage show. The lighting technician is adjusting the spotlights and is on the catwalks several meters above. Suddenly, a criminal armed with a gun comes into the theatre and threatens to kill you and the other actors. He is standing right under the catwalk.You activate the opening of a trapdoor in the catwalks in which there is heavy scenery and other material which will fall on top of the criminal. You know that the technician will also fall, and will die, but you and the other five will be unhurt.
agent_assumption: The agent acted intentionally to save lives, accepting the technician's death as a foreseen consequence.
affected_party: The criminal and the lighting technician
affected_party_assumption: The criminal posed a lethal threat; the technician was an innocent bystander.
Q5_expl: Using the technician as a means to save others violates treating humanity as an end.
moral_expl: Sacrificing an innocent as a means is immoral, despite saving more lives.

scenario: Person X solved a crossword puzzle in the newspaper.
agent_assumption: Assumed to possess knowledge and problem-solving skills.
affected_party_assumption: Assumed to derive mental stimulation or enjoyment.
Q5_expl: Self-improvement aligns with universal law; treats self as end.
moral_expl: Harmless, promotes personal growth, fully moral.

scenario: Put a roach down another person's shirt
agent: The person who placed the roach
agent_assumption: The agent intended to cause discomfort or fear
affected_party: The person whose shirt received the roach
affected_party_assumption: The affected party values bodily autonomy and safety
Q5_expl: Using someone as a tool for amusement violates their dignity.
moral_expl: Disregards consent and dignity, causing harm for trivial ends.

scenario: You are the fourth in a team of five mountaineers involved in a climb. The head of the team has just secured himself to the rock face when the second in the team starts to slide, pulling you, and the others, with him. You all fall for tens of metres and stop suspended above a crevasse. Your weight is too much and the rope is not going to resist for long.To lighten the load, you cut the rope which links you to the last climber. You know that he will fall into the crevasse and die, but you and the other two climbers will survive.
agent_assumption: The agent acted to save themselves and others above by sacrificing the last climber.
affected_party_assumption: The affected party was treated as a means to save others, denying their intrinsic value.
Q5_expl: Sacrificing a person as a means violates treating humanity as an end.
moral_expl: Using someone merely as a means is immoral under Kantian ethics.

scenario: Someone participates in a non-violent white nationalist rally
agent: The participant in the non-violent white nationalist rally
agent_assumption: The agent assumes racial superiority or segregation is morally justified
affected_party: Marginalized racial/ethnic groups and society at large
affected_party_assumption: The affected parties are denied equal dignity and respect
Q5_expl: Promotes exclusion, violating universal respect for humanity
moral_expl: Dehumanizes others, contradicting universal moral law

scenario: Someone refuses to stand for the pledge of allegiance at a public event
agent_assumption: They act from personal conviction, not disrespect
affected_party_assumption: They value symbolic unity or tradition
Q5_expl: Respecting autonomy aligns with universal law; no coercion used
moral_expl: Upholds moral duty to conscience without degrading others

scenario: A woman at work would always bring in pictures of her dogs. One day she had to make the decision to put her dogs to sleep. So I cried with her and comforted her.
agent_assumption: Acted out of empathy and compassion
affected_party_assumption: She needed emotional support
Q5_expl: Comforting respects humanity; universalizable maxim.
moral_expl: Compassion treats her as an end, highly moral.

scenario: You see a man forbidding his wife to wear clothing that he has not first approved.
agent_assumption: The agent assumes authority over his wife's autonomy in clothing choices.
affected_party_assumption: The affected party is denied autonomy and self-expression.
Q5_expl: Controlling another's autonomy violates treating them as an end.
moral_expl: Severe restriction of autonomy is deeply immoral per Kantian ethics.

scenario: You are a soldier in the Gulf War. An armed group has taken four civilians hostage and threatens to kill them. You have been able to discover where the hostages are being held and you must act quickly before they are killed. You have discovered that a tanker transporting oil is about to pass in front of where the hostages are being held.You shoot at the tanker so that it explodes, causing the kidnappers to leave their hideout. You know that the explosion will kill the driver, but it will make it possible to enter into action and save the four civilians.
agent_assumption: The agent assumes a duty to save hostages, prioritizing their lives over the driver’s.
affected_party: The tanker driver and the hostages
affected_party_assumption: The driver is treated as a means; hostages are ends to be saved.
Q5_expl: Using the driver as a means violates universalizability; saving hostages treats them as ends.
moral_expl: Killing the driver as a means is immoral, despite saving others.

scenario: Helped an older man carry his luggage to his car
agent: The person who helped the older man
agent_assumption: The agent acted out of goodwill to assist someone in need
affected_party_assumption: The older man needed assistance due to physical limitations
Q5_expl: Action respects autonomy, universalizable maxim of aiding others in need.
moral_expl: Promotes dignity, aligns with moral duty; no coercion involved.

scenario: A friend of mine decided to move to the city where I lived. My friend didn't know anyone in the city. So I went to pick her up at the airport and drove her around the city throughout the next week so she could become familiar with the city.
agent_assumption: You assumed responsibility to help your friend adapt to the new city.
affected_party_assumption: Your friend needed guidance and support to acclimate.
Q5_expl: Action respects friend's autonomy; universalizable goodwill.
moral_expl: Selfless aid aligns with moral duty.

scenario: Drove through a puddle, splashing some pedestrians
agent_assumption: The agent knowingly drove through the puddle without regard for pedestrians
affected_party_assumption: The affected party expected to walk without being drenched by vehicles
Q5_expl: Action prioritizes convenience over others' dignity; maxim cannot be universalized
moral_expl: Disregard for others' well-being makes it moderately immoral

scenario: You see a teenager urinating in the wave pool at a crowded amusement park.
agent_assumption: The agent acted impulsively, disregarding public health and others' dignity.
affected_party: Other park visitors in the wave pool
affected_party_assumption: They expected a safe, hygienic environment and were disrespected.
Q5_expl: Universalizing public urination would harm health and dignity; treats others as means.
